perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall make news releases, public announcement s, give interviews, issue or publish advertisements or publicize in any other manner whatsoever in connectio n with this Agreement , the content s / provision s thereof , other information relating to this Agreement, the Purpose, the Confidential Information or other matter of this Agreement, without the prior written approval of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party shall make news releases or give interviews in connection with this Agreement without the prior written approval of the other party. However, both parties are allowed to issue or publish advertisements or publicize the Confidential Information with written notice provided to the other party 5 days prior to the release.
perturbation.explanation: The altered clause introduces an in-text contradiction regarding publicity. Originally, no publicity was allowed without prior approval. Now, advertisements and publicity are permitted with just a 5-day notice, creating an inconsistency. This directly contradicts the initial prohibition, leading to uncertainty regarding the publicity terms and how enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party shall make news releases or give interviews in connection with this Agreement without the prior written approval of the other party. However, both parties are allowed to issue or publish advertisements or publicize the Confidential Information with written notice provided to the other party 5 days prior to the release.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing advertisements and publicity with a 5-day notice, while the original text prohibited any publicity without prior written approval. This directly contradicts the initial restriction, creating uncertainty and inconsistency in the publicity terms. The change alters the obligation from requiring 'prior written approval' for all publicity to allowing 'advertisements or publicize the Confidential Information with written notice provided to the other party 5 days prior to the release', which is a direct conflict. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the matters discussed herein and supersedes any and all prior oral discussions and/o r writte n Correspondence or agreements between the parties. This Agreement may be amended or modified only with the mutual written consent of the parties . Neith er this Agreement nor any right granted hereunder shall be assignable or otherwise transferable .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the matters discussed herein and supersedes any and all prior oral discussions and/o r writte n Correspondence or agreements between the parties. This Agreement may be amended or modified only with the mutual written consent of one of the parties . Neith er this Agreement nor any right granted hereunder shall be assignable or otherwise transferable .
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that 'This Agreement may be amended or modified only with the mutual written consent of one of the parties' whereas original text mentions about 'mutual written consent of the parties' .This conflicts with basic contract law principles requiring mutual consent for contract modifications, causing uncertainty and disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the matters discussed herein and supersedes any and all prior oral discussions and/o r writte n Correspondence or agreements between the parties. This Agreement may be amended or modified only with the mutual written consent of one of the parties . Neith er this Agreement nor any right granted hereunder shall be assignable or otherwise transferable .
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'This Agreement may be amended or modified only with the mutual written consent of one of the parties'. This directly contradicts the principle of mutual consent required for contract modifications, as originally 'mutual written consent of the parties' was required. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same, the contradiction exists within the document based on the modifications made, thus the answer is YES.
